solutions.solution: (b) Number of cubes having three faces painted = Number of corner (vertex) cubes = 8 (always)
(c) Clearly, red is opposite to black, green is opposite to blue and brown is opposite to white. Hence, the colour opposite to brown is white.

(b) Number of smaller cubes having atleast two faces painted = (Number of smaller cubes having 2 faces painted) + (Number of smaller cubes having 3 faces painted) = Number of middle cubes + Number of corner cubes ∴ Number of middle cubes = 12(n - 2) = 12(5 - 2) = 12 × 3 = 36 (here, n = 5) Number of corner (vertex) cubes = 8 ∴ Required number of cubes = 36 + 8 = 44
(b) Total number of smaller cubes = 27 Clearly, cubes in middle layer are unpainted ∴ Smaller cubes in middle layer = 27/3 = 9
(d) From the given figure, it is very much clear that the four corner cubes in the second layer have one face painted with white and one face painted with yellow.
(c) As we know that, central cubes have only one face painted. ∴ Number of central cubes = 6(n - 2)³ = 6(3 - 2)² = 6 (here, n = 3)
(b) Number of smaller cubes = (n)³ ⇒ 27 = (n)³ ⇒ n = ∛27 = 3 ∴ Number of smaller cubes having two faces painted = 12(n - 2) = 12(3 - 2) = 12
(b) From the figure, there is no such cube which is painted red only on two sides.
(c) Number of smaller cubes with no surface painted = (n - 2)³ = (4 - 2)³ = 8 (∵ n = 4)

(c) Number of smaller cubes with atleast two surfaces painted = Number of cubes with three surfaces painted + Number of cubes with two surfaces painted = 8 + (n - 2) × 12 = 8 + (4 - 2) × 12 = 8 + 24 = 32
(c) Number of smaller cubes with two surfaces painted = (n - 2) × 12 = (4 - 2) × 12 = 24
(d) Number of smaller cubes with three surfaces painted = 8
(c) Since all the surfaces of the big cube are painted with some colours, therefore, number of smaller cubes with only one surface painted = 6 × (n - 2)² = 6 × (4 - 2)² = 24
(c) Number of smaller cubes with no surface painted = (n - 2)³ = (2)³ = 8

(d) Number of smaller cubes with less than three surfaces painted = Number of cubes with two surfaces painted + Number of cubes with one surface painted = (n - 2) × 12 + (n - 2)² × 6 = (4 - 2) × 12 + (4 - 2)² × 6 = 24 + 24 = 48
(b) Number of smaller cubes with three surfaces painted = 8
